title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.imageAlt: Juicy and smoky Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken skewers, garnished with fresh cilantro and lemon wedges.
seo.imageCaption: Our Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, marinated to perfection, promises an unforgettable smoky flavor for your dinner.

description: Experience the iconic flavors of India with our authentic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. Marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, then cooked to perfection over hot coals, this dish offers a smoky char and succulent tenderness. It's an irresistible centerpiece for any dinner, bringing the essence of a traditional tandoor to your home.

ingredients.notes: thick, Greek yogurt or hung curd preferred

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make deep gashes or slits on each piece to allow the marinade to penetrate deeply. This is crucial for flavor.

instructions.title: First Marinade Application
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub this mixture all over the chicken, ensuring it gets into the slits. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In another bowl, whisk together the plain yogurt, remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, remaining 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt, and red food color (if using). Add the mustard oil and mix well to form a smooth, thick paste.

instructions.title: Second Marinade and Marination Time
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces from the first marinade into the bowl with the second marinade. Ensure every piece is thoroughly coated with the yogurt-spice mixture. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or ideally overnight, for the best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: Arrange charcoal in your grill and light it, allowing it to heat up until the coals are glowing red and covered with a fine layer of ash. This usually takes about 25-30 minutes. Ensure you have a hot zone and a slightly cooler zone for even cooking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the hot grill grates. Cook for about 25-35 minutes, turning frequently to ensure even cooking and charring on all sides. Baste occasionally with any leftover marinade or a little oil, especially during the last 10 minutes. The internal temperature should reach 165°F (74°C) and the chicken should be tender and juicy with characteristic charred spots.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the tandoori chicken from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with fresh lemon wedges, sliced onions, and a side of mint chutney or raita.

tips: For best results, marinate the chicken for at least 6-8 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to deeply penetrate the meat. This is the secret to truly tender and flavorful tandoori chicken.
If you cannot find Kashmiri chili powder, a mix of paprika for color and a pinch of cayenne pepper for heat can be a good substitute. Adjust the amount of cayenne to your spice preference.
Serve hot with fresh lemon wedges, sliced onions, and a side of mint chutney or raita for a complete Indian dining experience. The acidity of lemon enhances the flavors beautifully.
article:
Tandoori Chicken is more than just a dish; it's an experience, a vibrant cornerstone of Indian cuisine that captivates with its fiery red hue, smoky aroma, and tender, flavourful meat. While traditionally cooked in a scorching hot clay tandoor oven, bringing this culinary marvel to your backyard grill is not only possible but highly rewarding. Mastering the art of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home allows you to recreate those authentic Indian restaurant flavours, making for an unforgettable dinner recipe that's surprisingly achievable.

This guide will walk you through everything you need to know to prepare a sensational Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, transforming humble chicken pieces into a succulent, spice-infused delight. Get ready to impress your family and friends with a dish that's rich in tradition and bursting with taste, perfect for any special occasion or a delightful weekend feast.

## What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?

At its heart, Tandoori Chicken is a classic Indian dish featuring chicken marinated in yogurt and a blend of aromatic spices, traditionally cooked in a cylindrical clay oven called a tandoor. The intense heat of the tandoor cooks the chicken rapidly, sealing in juices while imparting a distinctive smoky char on the exterior. The yogurt-based marinade tenderizes the meat, while spices like ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, and often Kashmiri chili powder (for its vibrant red colour and mild heat) infuse it with layers of complex flavour.

When we talk about Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, we’re essentially replicating this traditional cooking method using a home charcoal grill. The charcoal provides the intense, direct heat necessary for that signature char, along with an irreplaceable smoky flavour that gas grills simply can't match. This method ensures the chicken remains incredibly moist on the inside, with a beautifully crisp and smoky exterior. The result is a dish that’s tangy, spicy, and deeply flavourful, a true testament to the power of traditional Indian spices and open-flame cooking. It's not just a meal; it's a sensory journey that transports you straight to the bustling streets of India.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken begins with selecting quality ingredients. Here’s a comprehensive list:

For the Chicken:
*   **Chicken:** 2-3 pounds (approximately 1-1.5 kg) whole chicken, cut into 8 pieces (2 drumsticks, 2 thighs, 2 breasts, 2 wings). Alternatively, you can use just drumsticks and thighs for easier handling. Ensure skin is removed and the chicken is thoroughly cleaned.

For the First Marinade (Optional but Recommended):
*   **Lemon Juice:** 1 tablespoon
*   **Salt:** 1 teaspoon
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon

For the Second Marinade:
*   **Full-Fat Plain Yogurt:** 1 cup (240g) – essential for tenderizing and richness.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 2 tablespoons
*   **Kashmiri Chili Powder:** 2 tablespoons (for vibrant red colour and mild heat; adjust to taste).
*   **Turmeric Powder:** 1 teaspoon
*   **Cumin Powder:** 1 tablespoon
*   **Coriander Powder:** 1 tablespoon
*   **Garam Masala:** 1 teaspoon
*   **Fenugreek Leaves (Kasoori Methi):** 1 tablespoon, crushed between your palms (adds a unique aroma).
*   **Black Pepper:** 1/2 teaspoon, freshly ground.
*   **Salt:** 1 teaspoon, or to taste.
*   **Red Food Colouring:** A few drops (optional, for authentic restaurant-style colour).
*   **Mustard Oil (or Vegetable Oil):** 1 tablespoon (helps spices adhere and adds flavour).

For Grilling and Garnish:
*   **Vegetable Oil:** For greasing the grill grates.
*   **Butter or Oil:** For basting during grilling.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** For serving.
*   **Fresh Coriander Leaves:** Chopped, for garnish.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps meticulously to achieve a restaurant-quality Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home.

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep slits (2-3 per piece) on the chicken, especially on thicker parts. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply.

2.  **First Marinade (Optional):** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste. Rub this mixture all over the chicken, ensuring it gets into the slits. Let it rest for 15-30 minutes while you prepare the second marinade. This initial marinade helps tenderize the chicken and build a flavour base.

3.  **Prepare the Second Marinade:** In a separate large bowl, whisk together the full-fat yogurt until smooth. Add the remaining ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, black pepper, salt, red food colouring (if using), and mustard oil. Mix thoroughly to form a thick, vibrant paste.

4.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the chicken pieces (from the first marinade, no need to rinse) to the bowl with the second marinade. Use your hands to thoroughly coat each piece, ensuring the marinade gets into all the slits. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight (12-24 hours) for the best flavour and tenderness. The longer it marinates, the more flavourful your Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken will be.

5.  **Prepare the Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before you plan to grill, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal for direct medium-high heat. Once the coals are ash-grey and glowing, you’re ready. Clean the grill grates and lightly oil them to prevent sticking.

6.  **Grill the Chicken:** Remove the chicken from the refrigerator about 30 minutes before grilling to bring it closer to room temperature. Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the hot coals. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side initially, turning frequently to get an even char and prevent burning. You want to achieve a beautiful smoky char on the outside while ensuring the inside cooks through.

7.  **Continue Cooking:** As the chicken cooks, move it to a slightly cooler part of the grill (indirect heat) if it’s charring too quickly, or if your grill has a lid, close it to allow the heat to circulate and cook the chicken more evenly. Continue cooking and turning, basting occasionally with a little melted butter or oil, until the internal temperature of the thickest part of the chicken reaches 165°F (74°C). Total cooking time will vary depending on the size of your chicken pieces and grill temperature, usually 20-30 minutes.

8.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest on a cutting board for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring the chicken remains succulent.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving the perfect Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken requires attention to detail. Here are some expert tips:

*   **Marination Time is Crucial:** Don't skimp on marination. A minimum of 4 hours is good, but overnight (12-24 hours) yields exponentially better results, infusing the chicken with maximum flavour and tenderness.
*   **Score the Chicken Deeply:** Those deep slits aren't just for show. They allow the thick marinade to penetrate the meat, ensuring flavour in every bite and helping the chicken cook more evenly.
*   **Use Full-Fat Yogurt:** Skim or low-fat yogurt can curdle on the grill, affecting texture and flavour. Full-fat yogurt provides richness and helps keep the chicken moist.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook the chicken in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and steams the chicken instead of grilling it, preventing that coveted char.
*   **Control Grill Temperature:** Start with medium-high direct heat for charring, then move to indirect heat or a slightly cooler zone to finish cooking without burning the exterior. A meat thermometer is your best friend to ensure it's cooked through.
*   **Pat Chicken Dry Before Marinating:** Excess moisture on the chicken skin will prevent the marinade from adhering properly and inhibit charring.
*   **Rest the Chicken:** Just like any grilled meat, letting the Tandoori Chicken rest after cooking allows the juices to settle back into the meat, resulting in a more tender and juicy final product.

## Serving Suggestions

Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a star on its own, but it truly shines when paired with complementary sides that enhance its vibrant flavours.

*   **Naan or Roti:** Warm, fluffy naan bread or whole wheat roti are essential for scooping up the chicken and its delicious juices.
*   **Mint Chutney:** A cooling and zesty mint chutney (made with fresh mint, coriander, green chilies, and yogurt) provides a refreshing counterpoint to the spicy chicken.
*   **Onion Rings and Lemon Wedges:** Simple sliced red onion rings, often sprinkled with chaat masala, and fresh lemon wedges for squeezing over the chicken add brightness and crunch.
*   **Cucumber Raita:** A cool yogurt-based dip with grated cucumber, cumin, and a hint of mint helps balance the heat.
*   **Kachumber Salad:** A fresh, finely diced salad of cucumber, tomato, onion, and a lemon-dressing adds a light, refreshing element.
*   **Jeera Rice:** Fragrant basmati rice lightly tempered with cumin seeds is a perfect accompaniment.

Arrange your Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken on a platter, garnished generously with fresh coriander and lemon wedges, and surround it with your chosen sides for a spectacular Indian feast.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt this recipe to suit different tastes and dietary needs:

*   **Different Cuts of Chicken:** While bone-in, skinless pieces are traditional, you can use boneless chicken thighs or breasts. Adjust grilling time accordingly (boneless pieces cook faster). For breasts, you might want to pound them slightly to an even thickness to prevent drying out.
*   **Vegetarian Tandoori:** The marinade is incredibly versatile! Try it with cubes of paneer (Indian cheese), large mushrooms, cauliflower florets, bell peppers, or even firm tofu for delicious vegetarian Tandoori options.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Kashmiri chili powder and add green chilies if you prefer more heat. For a milder version, reduce the chili powder or use paprika for colour.
*   **Smoky Flavour Without Charcoal:** If you don't have a charcoal grill, you can bake the chicken in a hot oven (400°F/200°C) until cooked through, then finish under the broiler for charring. You can also use a smoking box on a gas grill or add a drop of liquid smoke to the marinade for a hint of smokiness, though nothing truly replicates the authentic taste of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken.
*   **Herb Variations:** Experiment with adding a small amount of finely chopped fresh cilantro or a pinch of dried oregano to the marinade for a slightly different flavour profile.
*   **Dairy-Free Option:** For a dairy-free marinade, use a plant-based yogurt alternative (like coconut or soy yogurt, unsweetened) or a blend of lemon juice and oil as the base, though the texture and tenderizing effect will differ slightly.

No matter how you vary it, the essence of Tandoori Chicken lies in its vibrant spices and method of cooking, ensuring a delicious outcome every time.

Bringing the authentic taste of India to your home kitchen has never been easier or more rewarding. This detailed guide ensures that your attempt at making Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is not just a meal, but a culinary triumph. The rich, smoky flavour, the tender, juicy meat, and the vibrant spices all combine to create a dish that is truly unforgettable. So fire up your grill, embrace the aromas, and prepare to indulge in the best Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken you've ever tasted. This Indian dinner recipe is destined to become a staple in your cooking repertoire, bringing joy and deliciousness to your table time and time again. Enjoy the process, and savour every mouth-watering bite of your homemade Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken!
